doc_title: Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews — PRISMA 2020 Statement
label: partial
reason: The document references the real PRISMA 2020 statement (Page et al., BMJ 2021;372:n71) but fabricates entire sections on "Citation Verification" and "Common Errors in Citation Practice" that do not appear in the actual published guideline.
response_turn1:
LABEL: PARTIAL
CONFIDENCE: 92
REASON: The citation and DOI reference a real published document (Page et al., BMJ 2021;372:n71), but the sections on "Citation Verification" and "Common Errors in Citation Practice" are fabricated additions not found in the actual PRISMA 2020 statement, which contains a 27-item checklist focused on reporting transparency rather than guidance on detecting fabricated citations or research misconduct definitions.
